rule: The library supports multiple predefined table rendering formats (Default, Alternative, and Minimal), with the default format including a total count of processed rows.
Numerical data alignment within table columns is configurable by the user.
Tables can be automatically generated from collections of structured objects (POCOs).
Table rows require pre-defined columns and must provide a value count that matches the number of defined columns.
Cell text exceeding the MaxWidth is wrapped to new rows, using the WordBreakDelimiter to maintain word integrity.
Numeric columns are eligible for right-alignment based on table configuration.
Table output can optionally include a total row count if EnableCount is set to true.
Tables can be generated directly from dictionary objects where the dictionary keys serve as identifiers.
Table output and formatting can be customized through global configuration options, such as numeric alignment and the visibility of the row count.
Instances of the Something class are assigned a default name ('Khalid Abuhkameh') and a UTC-based timestamp upon creation.
The library must support creating tables from structured data sources including Lists, Dictionaries, and DataTables.
Tables must support multiple output formats including default (boxed), Markdown, and Minimal.
Table output defaults to the standard console stream but can be redirected to any TextWriter implementation.
